data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Spotify uses microservices to manage its large and complex application. This approach allows for scalability and independent deployment of components.
data.technical_spec: Deployed on cloud platforms, with each microservice handling a specific function, connected via APIs.

data.title: Distributed Systems
data.description: Spotify employs distributed system design to handle millions of user requests concurrently and provide seamless streaming experiences.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes load balancing and data replication for reliability and speed.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: Real-time data processing and user interaction is facilitated through event-driven architecture, ensuring fast response to user actions.
data.technical_spec: Relies on message brokers and scalable event streams.

data.title: Data-Intensive Applications
data.description: The application is designed to efficiently manage and process large volumes of music data and user interaction.
data.technical_spec: Employs big data technologies and optimized data storage solutions.

data.title: High Availability
data.description: Architectural strategies ensure the application remains available even during high traffic or in case of infrastructure failures.
data.technical_spec: Implements redundancy and automatic failover mechanisms.
